checks.a_analysis: The output text has been compared to the source document image across all ten pages. All paragraphs, headings, and lists present in the output correctly correspond to the text visible in the source document. No invented section headings or hallucinated body text was found. Expected AI-generated additions such as image alt text and titles are present and permissible.

The output includes phone numbers (352-672-4039, 919-869-4013) and references to various organizations. These numbers were checked against page 2 of the source document and found to be accurate. No emails or URLs were fabricated in the text.

The source document contains 10 pages, and the output correctly reflects 10 pages in its metadata and covers the full range of content. All 14 numbered sections and the final 'Roster of Roll Call Votes' are present in the output. The tables on pages 1, 2, and 10 are also included.

The tables in the output were compared to the source document. The Page 1 table (NC 911 Board Members/Staff) correctly contains all 28 expected data rows. The Page 2 table (Guests) matches the row count of the original. The Page 10 table (Roll Call Vote) correctly lists all 15 board members. No significant row loss is observed.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The content of the individual sections was reviewed. Key details, such as financial figures in section 4, specific PSAP migration details in section 5, and the detailed program descriptions in section 7 and 12, are all preserved without significant omission.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text was scanned for meaningful discrepancies in numbers, dates, and names. All financial values (e.g., $78,242,902, $81,073.20, $21,776,154) and statistics (e.g., population 10,546,429) match the source document exactly. The wording used preserves the original meaning throughout.

checks.a_analysis: The conversion results were reviewed for any duplicated content. While the headers for 'North Carolina 911 Board Meeting Roll Call' appear twice (reading_order 2 and 9), this corresponds to their appearance on successive pages in the source PDF (pages 1 and 2), which is an intentional repetition in the source. No other instances of duplicated paragraphs or sections were found.

The heading hierarchy is inconsistent across peer sections. Major sections 7, 8, and 10 are assigned header level 2 (H2), while major sections 11 and 12 are assigned header level 3 (H3). In the source document, these are all parallel top-level numbered sections. This inconsistency breaks the logical navigation structure for screen readers.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF on page 7 lists '11. Standards Committee Report' and '12. Technology Committee Report' as top-level numbered headings, identical in format to sections 7, 8, and 10.
checks.b_evidence_output: "type": "heading", "level": 3, "text": "11. Standards Committee Report" (reading_order 60) and "type": "heading", "level": 3, "text": "12. Technology Committee Report" (reading_order 63).
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 11 and 12, elements with reading_order 60 and 63.

checks.a_analysis: The tables in the document, including the complex 'Roll Call Vote' table on page 10, have been faithfully converted. Headers are correctly identified, and the grid structure (columns and rows) matches the source. Specifically, the conversion correctly preserved the 9-column structure of the final vote table.

The reading order is significantly compromised due to the omission of large sections of structural content. In section 4 (Consent Agenda), items 'a' and 'f' are missing. More critically, in section 5 (Executive Director Report), sub-items 'a' through 'd' and the first half of item 'e' are missing entirely. This causes the text to jump from the heading directly into the middle of a sentence on the next page, fundamentally breaking the document's flow.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 3 of the PDF contains text for Consent Agenda items a and f, and Executive Director Report items a, b, c, d, and the beginning of e ('Richmond Community College... On October 4, an official').
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON omits these sections, jumping from '5. Executive Director Report –' (reading_order 24) to 'announcement will be made...' (reading_order 29).

checks.a_analysis: Lists are generally converted correctly. While some lists are split by intervening paragraphs (such as in section 10), the items are accurately captured as list elements and the sequence remains logical relative to the source.

No fabricated or broken links were detected in the processed text. Although some URLs present in the missing sections of the PDF are not in the JSON, this is a result of the reading order/content omission defect rather than a link injection issue.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output faithfully reproduces the visual organization of the source PDF. The document begins with the meeting header, followed by the roll call tables for board members, staff, and guests. The meeting minutes follow the same numbered structure (1-14) as the source. The 'Roster of Roll Call Votes' table is placed correctly at the end. The presence of a table of contents and navigation elements is an expected accessibility feature.

All tables in the document, including the roll call tables on pages 1 and 2 and the complex 'Roll Call Vote' table on page 10, are rendered correctly in the HTML. Borders are present, columns are aligned, and headers are distinct. The rotated text in the source headers for the final table has been straightened for better readability in HTML, which is an acceptable visual difference.

The meaningful logos (NC DIT 911 Board) present in the source PDF are correctly rendered in the HTML version. The red 'APPROVED BY NC 911 BOARD' watermarks are decorative and their removal is an intentional accessibility improvement according to the evaluation criteria.

A review of the HTML rendering confirms that no body content (paragraphs, tables, or sections) is duplicated. Section headings appear in both the navigation pane and the document body as intended for accessibility.
